topics.hook: An owl can turn its head almost all the way around.
topics.core_fact: Owls rotate their heads up to ~270 degrees thanks to extra neck vertebrae and special blood-vessel adaptations.
topics.visual: Owl doing the head rotation. The motion is the whole video. Perfect for I2V.
topics.risk: Low. This is the most natively-animated topic in the batch.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: The best color-changer on Earth is colorblind.
topics.core_fact: Cuttlefish are colorblind yet produce dazzling color and pattern displays across their skin.
topics.visual: Cuttlefish rippling color waves across its body. Mesmerizing, loopable.
topics.risk: Low.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: This jellyfish can skip death.
topics.core_fact: Turritopsis dohrnii can revert from adult back to its polyp stage, effectively restarting its life cycle.
topics.visual: Drifting translucent jellyfish, glow, slow pulse. Strong loop-the-hook potential.
topics.risk: Low.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: An octopus has three hearts. And blue blood.
topics.core_fact: Octopuses have three hearts (two gill, one systemic) and copper-based hemocyanin makes their blood blue.
topics.visual: Live octopus, color/texture shifts, close eye contact. Caption carries the number.
topics.risk: Low. Do not attempt an internal-heart cutaway; show the animal and let the caption state the fact.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: Flamingos are not born pink.
topics.core_fact: Flamingo chicks are grey; the pink comes from carotenoid pigments in the shrimp and algae they eat.
topics.visual: Grey chick beat, then vivid pink adult beat. The color shift IS the payoff. Two clean stills.
topics.risk: Low. Strong say-dog-see-dog.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: A platypus glows in the dark.
topics.core_fact: Platypus fur is biofluorescent, glowing blue-green under ultraviolet light.
topics.visual: Normal platypus beat, then the same animal glowing blue-green under UV. Striking payoff.
topics.risk: Low. The UV-glow shot is the hero; make that plate the highest-quality one.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: A narwhal’s horn is actually a tooth.
topics.core_fact: The narwhal’s tusk is an elongated canine tooth with millions of nerve endings, used as a sensor.
topics.visual: Narwhal surfacing, tusk hero shot, arctic water.
topics.risk: Low.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: In seahorses, the dad gets pregnant.
topics.core_fact: Male seahorses carry the eggs in a brood pouch and give birth to the young.
topics.visual: Seahorse with full pouch, then releasing tiny young. Kid-friendly wonder.
topics.risk: Low. Birth-release beat is showable without anatomy cutaway.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: This animal can regrow its own brain.
topics.core_fact: Axolotls regenerate limbs, spinal cord, heart tissue, and parts of their brain.
topics.visual: Pink axolotl, gilly frills, smiling face. Very photogenic, strong prior.
topics.risk: Low. Show the animal, not a regrowth time-lapse diagram.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: A starfish has no brain. And no blood.
topics.core_fact: Sea stars have no brain and no blood; they move using a water vascular system that pumps seawater through their bodies.
topics.visual: Starfish underwater, tube feet moving, slow crawl on rock.
topics.risk: Low.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: Crows remember your face. And they hold grudges.
topics.core_fact: Crows recognize individual human faces and can remember a threatening person for years, even warning other crows.
topics.visual: Crow tilting head, sharp eye contact, group of crows. Slightly menacing-but-kid-safe.
topics.risk: Low.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: Reindeer eyes change color in winter.
topics.core_fact: Reindeer eyes shift from gold in summer to blue in winter, improving low-light vision in the dark arctic season.
topics.visual: Gold-eye summer beat, blue-eye winter beat. Color shift is the payoff.
topics.risk: Low.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: A sloth can hold its breath longer than a dolphin.
topics.core_fact: By slowing its heart rate, a sloth can suppress breathing for up to ~40 minutes, beating a dolphin’s typical dive.
topics.visual: Sloth moving slowly through canopy, underwater sloth swim if available.
topics.risk: Low.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: Koala fingerprints look almost exactly like yours.
topics.core_fact: Koala fingerprints are so similar to human ones they can be hard to tell apart, even under a microscope.
topics.visual: Koala beat, then macro of the koala’s fingertip ridges. The macro is the payoff.
topics.risk: Low.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: This bird can perfectly copy a chainsaw.
topics.core_fact: The superb lyrebird mimics complex sounds including chainsaws, camera shutters, and car alarms.
topics.visual: Lyrebird displaying. Audio is the hero here, lean on George VO plus mimic SFX. Strong loop/replay driver.
topics.risk: Low. This one’s payoff is sonic, so nail the audio design.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: Hippos sweat their own sunscreen.
topics.core_fact: Hippos secrete a reddish fluid often called blood sweat that acts as a natural sunscreen and antibiotic.
topics.visual: Hippo in sun, reddish sheen on skin. Avoid making it look like injury; frame as glistening.
topics.risk: Low-medium. Keep it clearly not-gory; the reddish sheen should read as a coating, not blood.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: A hummingbird’s heart beats over 1,200 times a minute.
topics.core_fact: In flight, some hummingbirds’ hearts beat more than 1,200 times per minute.
topics.visual: Hummingbird hover/feed (WAN), plus optional subtle pulsing-rhythm overlay or audio heartbeat to sell the number.
topics.risk: Low-medium. The number needs a felt beat; use audio/rhythm, not an anatomy cutaway.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: An electric eel can stun a horse.
topics.core_fact: Electric eels can discharge up to ~600 volts, enough to incapacitate large animals.
topics.visual: Eel (WAN) plus a discharge flash/spark effect beat (light MG/compositing). The shrimp water flash lane is a good reference.
topics.risk: Medium. The spark effect is the risk surface; reuse whatever worked on the shrimp flash beat.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: Lightning is five times hotter than the sun.
topics.core_fact: A lightning bolt can reach ~30,000 K, roughly five times the temperature of the sun’s surface (~5,500 K).
topics.visual: Lightning footage plus a sun-comparison beat. Phenomenon, not animal.
topics.risk: Medium. The comparison beat is where the MG lane gets tested. Good cohort candidate.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.

topics.hook: There are more trees on Earth than stars in our galaxy.
topics.core_fact: Earth has an estimated ~3 trillion trees; the Milky Way holds an estimated 100-400 billion stars.
topics.visual: Forest beat to galaxy beat, scale comparison. Pure MG/scale storytelling.
topics.risk: Medium-high. Hardest to make not feel like a slideshow; explicit MG-lane stress test, not safe-queue filler.
topics.production_rule: Higgsfield free/Ultra source-frame sweep first; no internal anatomy cutaway as core visual; captions carry numbers when needed.
